[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 然孰敢倡者 - Rhetorical question meaning 'who would dare to initiate it?' used to emphasize the lack of bold action.
  • 彼二执政亦知之而不敢言且不敢为也 - Indicates that the two officials know the situation but are too afraid to speak or act.
  • 二十有一日忽闻执政召在京父老士庶计事诣都堂 - Describes an event where officials summoned elders and commoners to the capital for a meeting.
  • 余同麻革潜众中以听 - Means the narrator and Ma Ge secretly listened among the crowd.
  • 二执政立都堂檐外 - Describes the two officials standing outside the eaves of the hall.
  • 杨居仁诸首领官从焉 - Indicates that Yang Juren and other leaders were present.
  • 省掾元好问宣执政所下令告谕 - Means the provincial clerk Yuan Haowen announced the officials' orders and instructions.
  • 且问诸父老便宜 - Means they asked the elders for advice.
  • 完顔努森拱立无语 - Describes Wan Yan Nusen standing silently.
  • 独完顔实讷阿巴决复申谕 - Indicates that only Wan Yan Shen Nei and Aba Jue reiterated the instructions.
  • 且继以泣涕 - Means they followed up with tears, emphasizing the emotional weight of the situation.
  • 诸愚叟或陈说细微不足采 - Describes the elders offering minor suggestions that are not significant.
  • 余语麻革将出而白前事 - Means the narrator told Ma Ge that they would report the previous events.
  • 革言莫若以奏说密陈子归草之吾当共上也 - Indicates that Ma Ge suggested drafting a secret report to be submitted.
  • 余以是退将明日同革献书 - Means the narrator decided to draft the report and submit it the next day with Ma Ge.
  • 其夕颇闻民间称有一西南崔都尉药招抚者将起事 - Describes hearing that a general named Cui, from the southwest, was planning to rebel.
  • 众皆曰事急矣安知无人 - Means the crowd said the situation was urgent and that no one knew what to do.
  • 余既归夜草书备论其事 - Describes the narrator drafting a detailed report at night.
  • 迟明怀以诣省庭且邀革徃自断此事系完顔氏存灭且以救余民虽死亦无愧矣 - Means the narrator planned to deliver the report early the next morning, inviting Ma Ge to join, and expressed their determination to save the people even if it meant death.
  • 是旦大 - Indicates that the next day, something significant happened.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 执政 - Refers to high-ranking officials in the government.
  • 在京父老士庶 - Refers to elders and commoners in the capital.
  • 都堂 - Refers to the central government office or hall.
  • 省掾 - Refers to a provincial clerk or official.
  • 完顔努森 - A specific official's name.
  • 完顔实讷阿巴决 - Names of other officials.
  • 杨居仁 - A leader's name.
  • 西南崔都尉药招抚者 - Refers to a general named Cui from the southwest who was planning to rebel.
  • 完顔氏 - Refers to the ruling family or clan.
  • 余民 - Refers to the common people.

Proper Nouns

  • 执政 - High-ranking officials.
  • 在京父老士庶 - Elders and commoners in the capital.
  • 都堂 - Central government office or hall.
  • 省掾 - Provincial clerk or official.
  • 完顔努森 - Specific official.
  • 完顔实讷阿巴决 - Other officials.
  • 杨居仁 - Leader.
  • 西南崔都尉药招抚者 - General Cui from the southwest.
  • 完顔氏 - Ruling family or clan.
  • 余民 - Common people.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 隂晦俄雨作 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'suddenly it turned overcast and began to rain.'
  • 余姑避民闾 - '姑' (gū) means 'temporarily' or 'for the time being,' and '避民闾' (bì mín lǘ) means 'to take refuge in a civilian home.'
  • 北兵入门矣 - '北兵' (běi bīng) refers to northern troops, likely referring to the Jurchen Jin forces. '入门矣' (rù mén yǐ) means 'have entered the city.'
  • 西南兵变已围尚书省矣 - '西南兵变' (xī nán bīng biàn) refers to a rebellion by southwestern troops. '已围尚书省矣' (yǐ wéi shàng shū shěng yǐ) means 'has already surrounded the Shangshu Province.'
  • 崔立为西面都尉权元帅 - '崔立' (Cuī Lì) is a proper name, and '西面都尉权元帅' (xī miàn dū wèi quán yuán shuài) means 'commander-in-chief of the western army.'
  • 举兵药安国 - '举兵' (jǔ bīng) means 'to raise an army,' and '药安国' (yào ān guó) is a proper name, likely referring to a person named An Guo.
  • 北方人素骁勇 - '北方人' (běi fāng rén) means 'northerners,' and '素骁勇' (sù xiāo yǒng) means 'naturally brave and courageous.'
  • 先以进 - '先' (xiān wéi) means 'to lead the way,' and '以进' (yǐ jìn) means 'to advance.'
  • 横刃入尚书省 - '横刃' (héng rèn) means 'to wield a sword,' and '入尚书省' (rù shàng shū shěng) means 'to enter the Shangshu Province.'
  • 二执政见而大骇 - '二执政' (èr zhí zhèng) refers to the two chief ministers, and '见而大骇' (jiàn ér dà hài) means 'saw and were greatly frightened.'
  • 汝辈有事当好议 - '汝辈' (rǔ bèi) means 'you people,' and '有事当好议' (yǒu shì dāng hǎo yì) means 'when there is something to discuss, you should do so properly.'
  • 吾为二执政闭门误众 - '吾' (wú) means 'I,' and '闭门误众' (bì mén wù zhòng) means 'closing the gates and misleading the people.'
  • 将饿死 - '将饿死' (jiāng è sǐ) means 'were about to starve to death.'
  • 今杀之以救一城民 - '今杀之' (jīn shā zhī) means 'now killing them,' and '以救一城民' (yǐ jiù yī chéng mín) means 'to save the people of the city.'
  • 且禁诸军士取民一钱处死 - '且禁' (qiě jìn) means 'and prohibit,' '诸军士' (zhū jūn shì) means 'all soldiers,' '取民一钱' (qǔ mín yī qián) means 'to take even one coin from the people,' and '处死' (chǔ sǐ) means 'to be executed.'
  • 阖郡称快 - '阖郡' (hé jùn) means 'the entire prefecture,' and '称快' (chēng kuài) means 'to cheer and rejoice.'
  • 以为有生路也 - '以为' (yǐ wéi) means 'to think,' and '有生路' (yǒu shēng lù) means 'to have a way to survive.'
  • 食时忽隂雨开霁 - '食时' (shí shí) means 'at mealtime,' '忽隂雨' (hū yīn yǔ) means 'suddenly it turned overcast and began to rain,' and '开霁' (kāi jì) means 'cleared up.'
  • 日光烂然 - '日光' (rì guāng) means 'sunlight,' and '烂然' (làn rán) means 'bright and radiant.'
  • 立提兵入宫 - '立提兵' (lì tí bīng) means 'Lì led his troops,' and '入宫' (rù gōng) means 'into the palace.'
  • 见太后自陈其事 - '见太后' (jiàn tài hòu) means 'to see the empress dowager,' and '自陈其事' (zì chén qí shì) means 'to explain the matter to her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北兵 - Likely refers to the Jurchen Jin forces, who invaded and eventually conquered the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 西南兵变 - Refers to a rebellion by southwestern troops, possibly during the late Northern Song period.
  • 尚书省 - The central government administrative body responsible for civil affairs during the Song Dynasty.
  • 崔立 - A historical figure who played a significant role in the fall of the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 药安国 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 太后 - Refers to the empress dowager, a powerful figure in the imperial court.

Proper Nouns

  • 崔立 - A historical figure who played a significant role in the fall of the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 药安国 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 实讷阿巴 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 努森 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 纳哈塔徳晖 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 杨居仁 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 聂天骥 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure involved in the events described.
  • 太后 - Refers to the empress dowager, a powerful figure in the imperial court.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惶怖 - Means 'fearful and terrified,' describing a state of extreme anxiety.
  • 听命 - Means 'to obey orders' or 'to follow instructions.'
  • 束手听命 - Describes a state of complete submission and obedience.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 左丞相都元帅 - Refers to high-ranking officials in the government, specifically the Left Chancellor and Grand Marshal.
  • 寿国公 - Refers to a title given to someone, likely a noble or high-ranking official.
  • 太后 - Refers to the Empress Dowager, a powerful figure in the imperial court.
  • 衞邸 - Refers to the Wei palace, likely a place of significant political importance.
  • 监国 - Refers to the act of governing in place of an absent monarch.
  • 北朝 - Refers to the Northern Dynasty, a historical period or political entity.
  • 女直 - Refers to the Jurchen people, an ethnic group that played a significant role in Chinese history.
  • 御史大夫 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the imperial court, specifically the Grand Censor.
  • 提防近侍局 - Refers to the Imperial Bodyguard Bureau, responsible for the safety of the emperor.
  • 左右司郎中 - Refers to officials in the Left and Right Secretariats, important administrative positions.
  • 户部尚书 - Refers to the Minister of Revenue, a high-ranking official in charge of financial matters.
  • 副元帅 - Refers to a Deputy Marshal, a military rank.
  • 监军 - Refers to a Marshal, a military commander.

Proper Nouns

  • 李新 - Refers to Li Xin, a historical figure.
  • 药安国 - Refers to Yao An Guo, a military leader.
  • 王守 - Refers to Wang Shou, a Marshal.
  • 费摩阿固岱 - Refers to Feimo Agudai, a high-ranking official.
  • 乌克逊努色尔 - Refers to Ukusun Nuerel, a high-ranking official.
  • 完顔仲平 - Refers to Wanyan Zhongping, a high-ranking official.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 都堂 - Refers to the central government or the capital city's administration.
  • 安国犯令 - Means 'An Guo violated the command.'
  • 叱左右斩以徇 - Means 'ordered the guards to behead him publicly.'
  • 朝士震悚 - Means 'the officials were terrified.'
  • 立以其弟某为平章政事 - Means 'the emperor appointed his younger brother as Chancellor of State Affairs.'
  • 张颂为殿前都防检 - Means 'Zhang Song was appointed as the Chief Inspector of the Palace Guards.'
  • 韩铎为副元帅知开封府 - Means 'Han Duo was appointed as Deputy Commander-in-Chief and Governor of Kaifeng Prefecture.'
  • 左司都事苏伯林济之为御史中丞 - Means 'Su Bolin Ji was appointed as the Deputy Director of the Left Office and Chief Censor.'
  • 吏部侍郎刘仲周谏议大夫张正伦参议省事 - Means 'Liu Zhongzhou, the Vice Minister of Personnel, and Zhang Zhenglun, the Councillor of State, were appointed as advisors to the provincial government.'
  • 梁王虽监国在宫中虚名而已立 - Means 'Emperor Liang, although nominally in charge of the state, was merely a figurehead.'
  • 鄂屯阿固岱为尚书右丞 - Means 'E Tun Agu Dai was appointed as the Right Vice Minister of the Board of Works.'
  • 温特赫伊实为参知政事 - Means 'Wen Te Her Yi Shi was appointed as the Vice Minister of State Affairs.'
  • 刁壁为兵部尚书元帅左监军 - Means 'Diao Bi was appointed as the Minister of War and Left Commander of the Army.'
  • 元好问为左右司员外郎 - Means 'Yuan Haowen was appointed as an Assistant Director of the Left and Right Offices.'
  • 省令史 - Refers to the officials in charge of the provincial government.
  • 立取仲周女为妻 - Means 'the emperor took Liu Zhongzhou's daughter as his wife.'

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 士大夫退闲者 - Refers to retired officials or literati, implying a group of individuals who have stepped down from official duties.
  • 皆以次迁擢台阁中 - Means 'all were promoted in turn to positions in the imperial government.' '迁擢' implies promotion, and '台阁' refers to the central government.
  • 其除拜无虚日 - Means 'there was no day without appointments,' indicating frequent promotions or appointments.
  • 俄立自为太师尚书令 - '俄立' suggests a sudden or hasty action, and '太师尚书令' refers to high-ranking officials, specifically the Grand Tutor and the Chief Secretary.
  • 郑王闻钧汝间有众据西山不从命 - '郑王' is a title or name, '钧汝间' refers to a region, and '据西山不从命' means 'rebelling and refusing to submit to orders.'
  • 立遣韩铎帅兵讨之 - '立遣' means 'immediately sent,' '韩铎' is a name, and '帅兵讨之' means 'to lead troops to suppress them.'
  • 铎中箭死 - '中箭死' means 'was killed by an arrow.'
  • 以折希顔知开封府 - '折希顔' is a name, and '知开封府' means 'to be appointed as the governor of Kaifeng Prefecture.'
  • 立又封诸内藏库 - '内藏库' refers to the imperial treasury, and '封诸' means 'to seal and store.'
  • 将以奉北兵 - '北兵' refers to the invading forces from the north, likely the Jurchens or Mongols.
  • 又搜选民间寡妇处女 - '寡妇处女' refers to widows and unmarried women, and '搜选' means 'to search and select.'
  • 亦将以奉北兵 - Repeated phrase, same meaning as above.
  • 然入其家者甚众 - '然' means 'however,' and '入其家者甚众' means 'many entered their homes.'
  • 又括刷在京金银 - '括刷' means 'to collect and confiscate,' and '在京金银' means 'gold and silver in the capital.'
  • 命百官分坊陌穷治之 - '命百官' means 'ordered the officials,' '分坊陌' means 'to divide into districts,' and '穷治之' means 'to thoroughly investigate.'
  • 贵人富家俱被害 - '贵人富家' refers to wealthy and influential families, and '俱被害' means 'all suffered harm.'
  • 陈国夫人王氏末帝姨也 - '陈国夫人' is a title, '王氏' is a surname, and '末帝姨也' means 'she was the aunt of the last emperor.'
  • 素富于财 - '素富于财' means 'was always wealthy.'
  • 平章巴萨夫人亦富侈 - '平章' is a title, '巴萨夫人' is a name, and '富侈' means 'wealthy and extravagant.'
  • 右丞李蹊旧以取积闻其妻子皆被搒掠拷讯死 - '右丞' is a title, '李蹊' is a name, '旧以取积' means 'used to accumulate wealth,' '闻其妻子皆被搒掠拷讯死' means 'it was known that his wife and children were beaten and interrogated to death.'
  • 立又自诣军前求免剽掠 - '自诣军前' means 'went to the front of the army,' '求免剽掠' means 'to request exemption from looting.'
  • 又求纵百姓出城挑菜充饥 - '纵百姓出城' means 'to allow the people to leave the city,' '挑菜充饥' means 'to gather vegetables to eat.'
  • 于是人得出近郊采蓬子窠甜苣菜杂米粒以食 - '蓬子窠甜苣菜' refers to various wild vegetables, and '杂米粒以食' means 'mixed with rice grains to eat.'
  • 又闻京西陈冈上有野麦甚丰 - '京西陈冈' refers to a place in the western part of the capital, and '野麦甚丰' means 'wild wheat was very abundant.'
  • 立又聚皇族皆入宫 - '聚皇族' means 'gathered the royal family,' and '入宫' means 'into the palace.'
  • 俄遣诣青城皆为北兵所杀 - '俄遣' means 'soon sent,' '诣青城' means 'to Qingcheng,' and '皆为北兵所杀' means 'all were killed by the northern soldiers.'
  • 如荆王梁王辈皆 - '如荆王梁王辈' means 'like King Jing and King Liang and others.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 士大夫 - Refers to scholar-officials, a key social class in imperial China.
  • 太师尚书令 - High-ranking officials in the imperial government.
  • 郑王 - A title or name, likely referring to a prominent figure.
  • 北兵 - Refers to the invading forces from the north, likely the Jurchens or Mongols.
  • 开封府 - The capital city of Kaifeng, a significant political and cultural center.
  • 内藏库 - The imperial treasury, a symbol of the state's wealth and power.
  • 陈国夫人 - A title, likely referring to a high-ranking noblewoman.
  • 平章 - A title, often referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 右丞 - A title, often referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 青城 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location.

Proper Nouns

  • 士大夫 - Scholar-officials.
  • 太师尚书令 - High-ranking officials.
  • 郑王 - A title or name.
  • 韩铎 - A name.
  • 折希顔 - A name.
  • 陈国夫人 - A title.
  • 王氏 - A surname.
  • 平章巴萨夫人 - A title and name.
  • 右丞李蹊 - A title and name.
  • 青城 - A place name.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 归潜志 - A title of a work, meaning 'Records of Returning to Seclusion.' It is a collection of historical anecdotes and biographies.
  • 钦定四库全书 - Refers to the 'Qinding Siku Quanshu,' the imperial edition of the Siku Quanshu, a massive collection of Chinese texts compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 录崔立碑事 - Means 'Recording the Story of the Stone Tablet Erected for Cui Li.' This phrase introduces the main subject of the text.
  • 崔立既变以南京降 - Means 'Cui Li, after changing his allegiance, surrendered Nanjing.' '既变' implies a significant shift in allegiance.
  • 自负其有救一城生灵功 - Means 'prided himself on saving the lives of the people in the city.' '自负' means 'to take pride in oneself.'
  • 汝等何时立一石书吾状耶 - Means 'When will you set up a stone tablet to record my deeds?' '汝等' is a respectful way of addressing a group.
  • 时立国柄入手 - Means 'At that time, he had control over the state.' '国柄' refers to the power of the state.
  • 生杀在一言 - Means 'Life and death were decided by a single word.' This phrase emphasizes Cui Li's absolute power.
  • 省庭日流血 - Means 'The court was filled with blood every day.' This vividly describes the violent atmosphere.
  • 上下震悚 - Means 'Those above and below were terrified.' '上下' refers to both the ruling class and the common people.
  • 诸在位者畏之 - Means 'All those in power were afraid of him.' '在位者' refers to officials and rulers.
  • 忽一省卒诣余家 - Means 'Suddenly, a provincial official came to my house.' '诣' means 'to visit' or 'to come to.'
  • 赍尚书礼房小帖子 - Means 'carrying a small note from the Ministry of Rites.' '赍' means 'to carry' or 'to bring.'
  • 首领官召赴礼部 - Means 'The head official summoned me to the Ministry of Rites.' '首领官' refers to a high-ranking official.
  • 余初愕然 - Means 'I was initially surprised.' '愕然' means 'to be startled' or 'to be surprised.'
  • 自以布衣不预事 - Means 'Thinking myself a commoner not involved in affairs.' '布衣' refers to a commoner or a person of low status.
  • 不知何谓 - Means 'I did not know what it was about.' '何谓' means 'what it means' or 'what it is about.'
  • 遇麻信之 - Means 'I met Ma Xinzhi.' '遇' means 'to meet.'
  • 昨日见左司郎中张信之言郑王碑事 - Means 'Yesterday, I saw Left Censor Zhang Xinzhi who said about the stone tablet for Prince Zheng.' '郑王' refers to Prince Zheng.
  • 欲属我辈作 - Means 'He wanted to entrust us to do it.' '属' means 'to entrust' or 'to assign.'
  • 即同入省礼房 - Means 'We then went together into the Ministry of Rites.' '同入' means 'together went into.'
  • 省掾曹益引见首领官张信之元裕之二人 - Means 'The deputy provincial official Cao Yi introduced us to the head officials Zhang Xinzhi and Yuan Yuzhi.' '省掾' refers to a deputy official.
  • 今郑王以一身救百万生灵 - Means 'Now, Prince Zheng saved the lives of millions of people with his own body.' '一身' means 'with his own body.'
  • 今在京官吏父老欲为立碑纪其事 - Means 'Now, officials, elders, and common people in the capital want to erect a stone tablet to record his deeds.' '在京' means 'in the capital.'
  • 众议属之 - Means 'The public opinion is in favor of it.' '属' means 'to belong' or 'to be in favor of.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 归潜志 - A historical work compiled by Liu Qi, a Yuan dynasty scholar, containing anecdotes and biographies of historical figures.
  • 钦定四库全书 - A monumental collection of Chinese literature compiled under the patronage of the Qing dynasty, including a vast array of texts from various periods.
  • 崔立 - A historical figure who played a significant role in the politics of the time, particularly during the Jin dynasty.
  • 南京 - Refers to the city of Kaifeng, which was the capital of the Jin dynasty and was often called 'Nanjing' (Southern Capital) to distinguish it from the Northern Capital, Zhongdu (modern Beijing).
  • 生杀在一言 - A phrase that highlights the absolute power of a ruler or official, where life and death decisions are made with a single word.
  • 省庭日流血 - Describes a period of intense political turmoil and violence, often associated with the fall of the Jin dynasty and the Mongol conquest.
  • 上下震悚 - Reflects the widespread fear and anxiety among both the ruling class and the common people during times of political instability.
  • 郑王 - Refers to Prince Zheng, a historical figure who is mentioned in the context of Cui Li's actions and the proposed stone tablet.

Proper Nouns

  • 归潜志 - The title of the work.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The title of the imperial edition of the Siku Quanshu.
  • 崔立 - A historical figure.
  • 南京 - The city of Kaifeng, often called 'Nanjing.'
  • 郑王 - Prince Zheng, a historical figure.
  • 张信之 - Left Censor Zhang Xinzhi, a historical figure.
  • 元裕之 - Head official Yuan Yuzhi, a historical figure.
  • 麻信之 - Ma Xinzhi, a historical figure.
  • 曹益 - Deputy provincial official Cao Yi, a historical figure.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 二君且已白郑王矣 - Formal statement indicating that the two gentlemen have already informed the King; '且已' implies the action has been completed.
  • 二君其无让余 - Polite request for the two gentlemen not to hesitate or refuse the speaker; '其' is a modal particle expressing a wish or command.
  • 祁辈布衣无职 - Humble self-description indicating that the speaker and others are commoners with no official position; '布衣' refers to ordinary citizens, and '无职' means having no official role.
  • 此事出于众心 - Indicates that the matter is driven by the collective will of the people; '出于' means 'arising from' or 'coming from.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '余平生有二乐曰良友曰异书' introduces the two joys of life, which are then elaborated upon in the following sentences.
  • The phrase '欣然忘寝食' describes the intense joy experienced when enjoying these joys.
  • The clause '从吾讲学见吾过失且笑谈游宴以忘忧' explains the dual role of good friends: teaching and providing social enjoyment.
  • The clause '资吾见闻助吾辞藻属文著论以有益' explains the benefits of reading rare books: enriching knowledge and improving writing skills.
  • The clause '彼酒色膏梁如一时浮云过目竟何所得哉' contrasts the fleeting nature of luxurious living with the lasting benefits of reading.
  • The clause '肥醲甘脆世所共珍使饱而遇之则食如泥土' uses a metaphor to describe the diminishing value of luxurious foods when they become too common.
  • The clause '藜藿葵荠世所共贱使饥而遇之则食如饴糖' uses a metaphor to describe the increased value of humble vegetables when they are needed.
  • The clause '贫穷之士自乐富贵之人亦有苦是则我辈区区以空乏为忧者悖矣' contrasts the perspectives of poor scholars and wealthy people, criticizing those who are overly concerned with material poverty.
  • The clause '国之不可治犹可以治其家人不能正犹能正其身' suggests that personal responsibility is important even if broader societal issues cannot be resolved.
  • The clause '虽未太平而吾一家独不可太平乎诚在我者也' questions the importance of personal peace in the face of broader turmoil, emphasizing personal responsibility.
